approach: city-wide daily total, Ridge regression, allocated to stations by historical share
rationale: Grid-zone and station-level forecasts were tested and abandoned first (too sparse / too noisy respectively). City-wide aggregation gave a forecastable signal (CV 0.19). XGBoost was tried first at city level but lost to the naive 7-day-average baseline by 20.4% — overfitting on 124 days of training data. Ridge regression on the same features generalizes better, beating the naive baseline by 9.0%.
